events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 19.0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Atmospheric oxygen concentration measured at 19.0%, which is below OSHA 1910.146 minimum of 19.5% for safe entry without continuous forced air ventilation or an approved supplied-air respirator."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately evacuate personnel from the confined space if entry has occurred.",
    "Do not permit entry until the space is ventilated and retested, showing oxygen at or above 19.5% and below 23.5%.",
    "If ventilation cannot raise oxygen to acceptable levels, implement fully compliant permit-required confined-space entry procedures with appropriate respiratory protection per OSHA 1910.146."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C) requires testing for oxygen content; an atmosphere below 19.5% is considered oxygen-deficient and is an IDLH hazard. The current reading of 19.0% renders the space immediately dangerous to life and health, stopping all entry work until corrected or properly controlled.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C) - Pre-entry testing for oxygen",
    "OSHA 1910.146(b) - Definitions: Hazardous atmosphere (O2 < 19.5% or > 23.5%)",
    "OSHA 1910.146(d)(2) - Permit-required confined space identification",
    "OSHA 1910.146 Appendix B - Procedures for Atmospheric Testing"
  ]
}
